title: Reis origins from Lilith and Yui is she aware of them
display_title: Reis origins from Lilith and Yui is she aware of them
wikitext:
[]: Rei's origins from Lilith and Yui: is she aware of them?             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Tue Jan 10, 2012 6:10 pm This may have been mentioned within another thread before, and if it is, I obviously did not identify it. Anyhow, I am unsure of how much Rei knows of her origins. She may be aware of her connection to Lilith, as some areas of her monolouge suggest that. She is aware of the fact that she can be replaced as she dies, as we know, but does she know of her connectons to Yui? In episode 15, Shinji speaks to Rei over her actions here: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Tue Jan 10, 2012 6:18 pm In general I do not believe that Rei is aware of her connection with Yui. As with her connection to Lilith, Rei I probably doesn't know, Rei II most likely does, Rei III knows. Rei III's certainty is shown by knowing what Gendo intends to do in EOE with instrumentality and what her role in the scenario is.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Tue Jan 10, 2012 6:40 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: In general I do not believe that Rei is aware of her connection with Yui. As with her connection to Lilith, Rei I probably doesn't know, Rei II most likely does, Rei III knows. Rei III's certainty is shown by knowing what Gendo intends to do in EOE with instrumentality and what her role in the scenario is.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Jan 10, 2012 7:28 pm I think you'd be embarrassed too if someone made the remark of you possibly being a good mother. I know I would (Gender unrelated).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jan 10, 2012 8:30 pm Oh, just look who made a thread about this very topic when they first joined: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/1888/Does-Rei-II-know-her-Identity/ And to the OP: Rei II knowing she's a Yui-clone is most likely an error on that analysis page. Thanks! KnightmareX13 wrote: In general I do not believe that Rei is aware of her connection with Yui. As with her connection to Lilith, Rei I probably doesn't know, Rei II most likely does, Rei III knows. Rei III's certainty is shown by knowing what Gendo intends to do in EOE with instrumentality and what her role in the scenario is.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:06 pm Yep, Rei I is just Lillith on vacation, may have known she was a clone of Yui but probably didn't give a shit about the details. Rei II spent her whole life in the dark on the subject, other than knowing she wasn't a 'real' human. Rei III seems to have pieced some of it together before instrumentality kicked off, at which point everything should have been made made obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by BulletShoot » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:41 pm I don't think that Rei would know her connections to Yui, based primarily on the fact that Gendo never talks about Yui except to Fuyutsuki and Shinji in the graveyard scene, and even then not very much. Also during said graveyard scene Gendo states he has no photos or the like of Yui so it seems unlikely that she could have just stumbled on this tantalizing piece of information by herself. So someone would have had to have told her; Fuyutsuki never even seemed to talk to her, Gendo almost certainly wouldn't have told her unless he thought it might help his grand scheme somehow, the only other person who might know of her connection to Yui is Ritsuko and she has no reason to tell Rei. The only thing that pops into my head that could contradict this train of thought is that in EoE Gendo tells her to take him to Yui in a manner that seems to suggest that she knows who that, but even that doesn't mean Rei would know how she is related to Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:55 pm It seems as if Rei three did know about Yui at least, because if she did not, she would not be aware of who Gendo was referring to in EoE, although it seems unlikely to me that she would of asked him who Yui was if she did not know. This is because we all know that Rei showed unwavering loyalty to Gendo, but on the other hand, Rei three did not. However, if Rei three did not know of Yui and Yui's death, it would of been easy enough for Gendo to say 'take me to my wife's side'. He did speak of Yui in a familiar way. But yes, I too doubt that Rei knew that she was a clone of Yui herself, but she was aware that she was not a true human. Also, Rei two and three knew better than to talk back to or contradict their superiors, but Rei one quite clearly did not, judging from her 'old hag' comments towards Naoko, which resulted in her death. I suppose she was only young, too small to know any better.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jan 10, 2012 11:02 pm ReiAyanami25 wrote: It seems as if Rei three did know about Yui at least, because if she did not, she would not be aware of who Gendo was referring to in EoE, although it seems unlikely to me that she would of asked him who Yui was if she did not know.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jan 11, 2012 2:33 pm I don't think she really knows, but is highly suspect that she's not right.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Wed Jan 11, 2012 4:04 pm ReiAyanami25 wrote: Rei two and three knew better than to talk back to or contradict their superiors, but Rei one quite clearly did not, judging from her 'old hag' comments towards Naoko, which resulted in her death. I suppose she was only young, too small to know any better.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Wed Jan 11, 2012 6:03 pm Naoko didn't know who Rei was when Gendo first brought her in. It was that she noticed Rei's resemblance to Yui that made her start investigating where Rei came from.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Wed Jan 11, 2012 7:00 pm JoeD80 wrote: Naoko didn't know who Rei was when Gendo first brought her in. It was that she noticed Rei's resemblance to Yui that made her start investigating where Rei came from.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:06 pm ReiAyanami25 wrote: Yes, you see this when she is searching for Rei's file, she states this: Naoko had a deep hatred for Yui, and when she encountered a child that strongly resembled Yui herself, it is no wonder that she began to search for answers. She must have found what she was seeking in the end, due to her statement that Rei one was replaceable. How she found the answers when Rei's files were empty, I do not know. Perhaps she found Rei's room and the clones in the depths of Central dogma, if they existed at that point in time. Maybe she managed to get the truth out of Gendo, and that was the possible reason that Gendo sent Rei, to put Naoko off working for him, or worse, to ensure that she killed herself so she would not spread the secret of Rei's creation. I am sure that it would cause a lot of disarray if it was known to everyone.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:27 pm ^ Just want to say that there are a number of people who think even Ritsuko does not know everything about Rei. She knows about the dummies but may not know Rei is part of Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu Jan 12, 2012 6:16 am symbv wrote: ^ Just want to say that there are a number of people who think even Ritsuko does not know everything about Rei. She knows about the dummies but may not know Rei is part of Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by BulletShoot » Fri Jan 13, 2012 4:30 pm A lot of questions in Evangelion seem like they could be answered if we could figure out exactly who knows what when.
summary: []: Rei's origins from Lilith and Yui: is she aware of them?             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Tue Jan 10, 2012 6:10 pm This may have been mentioned within another thread before, and if it is, I obviously did not identify it. Anyhow, I am unsure of how much Rei knows of her origins. She may be aware of her connection to Lilith, as some areas of her monolouge suggest that. She is aware of the fact that she can be replaced as she dies,
